A: The expiration date for each discount will appear on the discount card once you press Unlock Code in the Available Offers window. 

Q: Are there any restrictions on the products I can purchase with the discount?

A: The 30% off discount code is valid on most products in the Logitech store, including sale items, with no blackout dates. Please note that the 30% off code excludes new products, the Pro X2 Superstrike mouse, and PRO Series Simulation items, and is limited to 3 items per order. However, a 5% discount code can be applied to new products and PRO Series Simulation products during the first six months following a product's release.

Q: Is there a limit to how many times I can use my discount code?

A: Each discount code can be used once, and you can only use one code per transaction on each website. However, you'll receive a new code each month your Nitro subscription is active.

Q: When exactly do new codes become available?

A: New discount codes become available at the beginning of each calendar month for active Nitro members.

Q: Can I use my code on any device?

A: Yes! Once you receive your code through the Discord desktop app, you can copy it and redeem it at the appropriate webstore on any device of your choice.

Q: Why does my discount code show as "unavailable"?

A: If you’re seeing “Unavailable”, that means there are issues generating discount codes. No action is needed on your end. Check back later to claim your code!

Unavailable Discount Code

Q: Why isn't my discount code working?

A: If you're experiencing issues with your Logitech discount code, contact their support team directly: https://support.logi.com/hc/en-us 

Q: I think I found a bug. How do I report it?

A: If you're experiencing technical issues or suspect you've encountered a bug, please reach out to Discord Support for help. We can investigate and assist you further.

articles.name: What is the Take It Down Act (TIDA)
articles.title: What is the Take It Down Act (TIDA)
articles.body:
The Take It Down Act (TIDA) is a law that allows certain individuals to request the removal of intimate images shared or created without their consent, sometimes referred to as “revenge porn” or “non-consensual pornography.” It requires covered platforms, like Discord, to provide a clear reporting process, review qualifying removal requests, remove covered content within a defined timeframe, and take reasonable steps to limit the spread of that content.

Your Rights and Our Responsibilities Under TIDA

If you are authorized to make a request under TIDA, you, or a representative acting on your behalf, may:

  • Notify Discord of any “non-consensual intimate visual depiction” published on Discord that depicts you and was published without your consent; and,
  • Submit a request for Discord to remove the depiction. For more details about how to submit a removal request, you can refer to our help article.

So that we can help you, please make sure that your TIDA removal request meets these requirements:

  • You are identifiable in the content or via the context in which it is displayed (for example, your face is visible or your name is mentioned);
  • The content shows you nude or engaged in sexual activity; and,
  • The content was published without your consent.

Discord may also action content submitted for removal through this process even if it does not meet the criteria under the Take It Down Act such as for violation of our Community Guidelines, or where other laws may apply.

Under TIDA, Discord, as a covered platform, is required to:

  • Maintain a clear and accessible process that allows a depicted individual (or their authorized representative) to request the removal of non-consensual intimate visual depictions;
  • Review each removal request and determine whether it satisfies the requirements of the law;
  • Remove the reported content within the 48 hour time period required by law when a valid request is received; and,
  • Make reasonable efforts to identify and remove known copies of the same non-consensual intimate visual depiction.

What to Expect After You Submit a Removal Request Under TIDA

We will promptly review your removal request to confirm that it meets the requirements of TIDA. If the request is complete and valid, we will remove the reported content within 48 hours. We will also make reasonable efforts to identify and remove known copies of the content.

If your submission is incomplete, we may be unable to process your request until sufficient information is provided.

articles.name: Nitro Rewards
articles.title: Nitro Rewards
articles.body:
Nitro is expanding its perks beyond Discord with Nitro Rewards, a third-party benefits program included with your membership. From games to gear, these are perks built for how you play.

What this article covers:

  • What are Nitro Rewards?
  • Xbox Game Pass 
  • Logitech G Discount
    • How it Works
    • Eligibility Requirements
    • How to Access Your Discount Code
  • SteelSeries Discount
    • How it Works
    • Eligibility Requirements
    • How to Access Your Discount Code
  • Frequently Asked Questions

What are Nitro Rewards?

Important: Nitro Rewards are rolling out to eligible members over the coming weeks. If you don't see Nitro Rewards in the Nitro tab yet, they haven't reached your account. All eligible Nitro members will have access soon. Perk availability varies by region. Nitro Basic and Nitro Classic members are not eligible.

Nitro Rewards is a benefits program included with your Nitro membership at no additional cost. It gives members access to perks from partners that matter to people who play games.

Nitro Rewards will evolve and change over time. You can check your Nitro Home to see what's currently available to you. As long as your Nitro membership is active, you'll have access to whatever perks are currently available in your rewards collection.

For a full list of the other perks you get with Nitro, check out What are Nitro & Nitro Basic? Article.

You can access Nitro Rewards through your Nitro Home tab in the Discord desktop app.

Nitro Home tab

Nitro Rewards Program Terms 

Nitro Rewards is included with eligible paid Nitro memberships at no additional cost, giving you access to perks from partners we think you'll actually use. The program is designed to grow and evolve — we'll be updating the partners and offers over time, and what's available may change.

If you are an eligible Xbox Game Pass subscriber, you get access to 3 Discord Nitro perks at no extra cost. For more info, check out this Help Center article: Taste of Nitro for Xbox Game Pass Subscribers.

Individual perks have their own eligibility conditions, redemption requirements, and availability limitations, which you'll find described in connection with each perk. Not all perks are available in all regions or to all subscription types. Nitro Basic and Nitro Classic members are not eligible for Nitro Rewards.

Perks provided through Nitro Rewards are supplementary to your core Nitro benefits and are offered in partnership with third parties. Discord isn't responsible for third-party products or services accessed through the program, and the third parties may impose their own terms, restrictions, and limitations.

Access to Nitro Rewards perks requires an active Nitro membership. Some perks, once claimed, may remain valid after your membership ends per the terms applicable to that perk.

Xbox Game Pass 

Take your gaming further with Xbox Game Pass, included with your Nitro membership. Whether you're looking for something new to play or want to stream your existing Xbox library, this benefit puts more gaming within reach — no extra subscription needed.

Your Nitro membership includes:

  • 50+ games like Stardew Valley, DayZ, and Fallout 4
  • 10 hours of cloud gaming each month
  • Play and earn up to $25 a year in the Store with Rewards

This benefit is available to active Nitro subscribers in eligible regions that do not have an existing Xbox Game Pass subscription.

Eligibility Requirements

You are eligible if:

  • You have an active Discord Nitro membership (through a paid membership, Nitro gift, and most Nitro trials)
  • You are not an existing Xbox Game Pass subscriber
  • You are located in an eligible region (see below)

You are not eligible if:

  • Using Nitro Credits without a Nitro subscription. 
  • You are a Nitro Classic or Nitro Basic member

Eligible regions for Xbox Game Pass (Starter Edition): Australia, Austria, Belgium, Canada, Czech Republic, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Hungary, India, Ireland, Italy, Japan, Mexico, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, South Korea, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, United Kingdom, United States

See Xbox Game Pass (Starter Edition) terms: https://www.xbox.com/legal/starter-edition-terms 

How to Access Xbox Game Pass

Tip: If you have an existing Xbox account connected to Discord but aren't seeing your Xbox Game Pass Nitro benefits, try re-linking your account. This can happen for some users with previously connected accounts. Re-linking should resolve the issue.

1. On desktop or browser, select the Direct Messages icon [] in the upper-left corner. 

2. Press the Nitro Home tab to navigate to the Nitro Rewards page. 

 

Direct Messages Icon (Left) and Nitro Tab (Right)

3. Locate the Xbox Game Pass card or banner and press Claim to open the Xbox Game Pass window.

Claim for free Xbox Game Pass card

5. Follow the prompts to activate your benefit and link your Microsoft account. 

Logitech G Discount  

Important: As of July 1, 2026, the KontrolFreek discount will no longer be available.The good news? We're continuing to work on new discounts to make your Nitro experience even more valuable. Thank you for your continued feedback and support!
Important: To receive your code, you must use the desktop app. Eligibility conditions apply, see below for more information.

How it Works

The following terms apply to this promotional offer:

  • Code details and restrictions: Discount codes are valid on most items in each store, including sale items, with no blackout dates. Please note that the Logitech 30% off code excludes new products, the new Pro X2 Superstrike mouse and PRO Series Simulation lines, and is limited to 3 items per order.
  • For new products and PRO series simulation gear items, a 5% discount is available on new products for the first 6 months following a product's release (limited to 1 use per customer). The PRO series simulation gear discount is rolling out to all users in the upcoming days.
  • Immediate availability: Codes become available to eligible users as soon as you subscribe to Nitro and must be claimed in your Nitro Home.
  • Fresh codes monthly: Each calendar month, you can claim a new discount code as long as this reward is available.
  • Code expiration: The code expiration dates can be found in the app. Codes will always expire on that date, no matter when they were granted.
  • No code stacking: Only one code can be used per transaction.
Note: If you cancel your Nitro subscription, you can still use any previously claimed discount codes until they expire, but you won't receive new codes until you resubscribe.

Eligibility Requirements

You are eligible:

  • With an active Discord Nitro subscription (through a paid membership, Nitro gift, and most Nitro trials)
  • If you’re located in a region that Logitech G ships to (see Logitech G regions for details)

You are not eligible if:

  • Using Nitro Credits without a Nitro subscription. 
  • You are a Nitro Classic or Nitro Basic subscriber.
Note: Once you unlock a discount code, you will not be eligible for a refund on your Nitro subscription. See our Refund Policy for more details.

How to Access Your Discount Code

1. On desktop or browser, select the Direct Messages icon [] in the upper-left corner. 

2. Press the Nitro Home tab to navigate to the Nitro page. 

 

Direct Messages Icon (Left) and Nitro Home Tab (Right)

3. Locate and select the Logitech G Discount card under the What’s New? section.

 Logitech G Discount Card

4. Press Unlock Rewards to open the Logitech G Discounts window and see your available codes. 

Unlock Rewards button

5. Select Unlock Code for your preferred discount to reveal your code—you can copy it immediately or return to view it later. 

Unlock Code button in Logitech G Discounts window

Using Your Discount Code

After claiming your discount code, here's how to use it on the Logitech G website:

  1. Visit the Logitech G website
  2. Add items to your cart 
  3. Proceed to checkout
  4. Enter your discount code in the promo code field
  5. Complete your purchase with the discount applied

SteelSeries Discount

Important: To receive your code, you must use the desktop app. Eligibility conditions apply, see below for more information.

How it Works

The following details apply to this promotional offer:

  • Minimum purchase requirements: You must make a minimum subtotal purchase of $20 to use your discount at SteelSeries. There is no minimum purchase requirement for KontrolFreek.
  • Code details and restrictions: Discount codes are valid on most items in each store, including all sale items, with no blackout dates. Please note that the SteelSeries code cannot be applied to the Arctis Nova Elite, Blemished Boxes, and is limited to 3 items per order.
  • Immediate availability: Codes become available to eligible users as soon as you subscribe to Nitro and must be claimed in your Nitro Home.
  • Fresh codes monthly: Each calendar month, you can claim a new discount code as long as this reward is available.
  • Code expiration: The code expiration dates can be found in the app. Codes will always expire on that date, no matter when they were granted.
  • No code stacking: Only one code can be used per transaction.
Note: If you cancel your Nitro subscription, you can still use any previously claimed discount codes until they expire, but you won't receive new codes until you resubscribe.

Eligibility Requirements

You are eligible:

  • With an active Discord Nitro subscription (through a paid membership, Nitro gift, and most Nitro trials)
  • If you’re located in a region that SteelSeries ships to (see SteelSeries regions for details)

You are not eligible if:

  • Using Nitro Credits without a Nitro subscription. 
  • You are a Nitro Classic or Nitro Basic subscriber.
Note: Once you unlock a discount code, you will not be eligible for a refund on your Nitro subscription. See our Refund Policy for more details.

How to Access Your Discount Code

1. On desktop or browser, select the Direct Messages icon [] in the upper-left corner. 

2. Press the Nitro Home tab to navigate to the Nitro page. 

 

Direct Messages Icon (Left) and Nitro Tab (Right)

3. Locate and select the SteelSeries x KontrolFreek Discounts card under either the NITRO REWARDS or the EXPLORE WHAT’S NEW section.

 SteelSeries x KontrolFreek Discounts Card

4. Press Take me there to open the Available Offers window and see your available discounts. 

Take me there Button

5. Select Unlock Code for your preferred discount to reveal your code—you can copy it immediately or return to view it later. 

Unlock Code button in Available Offers window

Using Your Discount Code

After claiming your discount code, here's how to use it on the SteelSeries or KontrolFreek website:

  1. Visit the SteelSeries store or KontrolFreek website
  2. Add items to your cart (minimum $20 purchase required on SteelSeries.com)
  3. Proceed to checkout
  4. Enter your discount code in the promo code field
  5. Complete your purchase with the discount applied

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: I'm a Nitro member but I don't see Nitro Rewards. Why? 

A: Nitro Rewards are rolling out gradually to members. If you don't see them in your Nitro Home yet, they haven't reached your account. All eligible Nitro members will have access over the coming weeks. You can check the eligibility conditions for each perk in the sections above.

Q: Can I contact support to get early access to Nitro Rewards? 

A: Our support team is not able to speed up the rollout or grant early access to individual accounts. You'll receive access once the rollout reaches your account.

Q: Are Nitro Rewards available in my country? 

A: Availability varies by perk. Each partner section above lists the eligible regions. If your country isn't listed for a specific perk, that perk is not currently available in your region.

Q: Do Nitro Rewards cost extra? 

A: No. Nitro Rewards are included with your Nitro membership at no additional cost. Your membership price stays the same.

Q: What happens to my Nitro Rewards if I cancel Nitro? 

A: You'll lose access to all Nitro Rewards perks when your membership ends. Any active partner benefits like Xbox Game Pass will also end.

Q: Do I need an existing Xbox or Microsoft account to use this benefit? 

A: Yes, you'll need a Microsoft account to redeem and access Xbox Game Pass (Starter Edition). If you don't have one, you can create one for free at microsoft.com.

Q: What happens to my Xbox Game Pass (Starter Edition) access if I cancel my Nitro membership? 

A: Your access to Xbox Game Pass (Starter Edition) is tied to your active Nitro membership. If you cancel, you will automatically lose access to this benefit at the end of your current billing period.

Q: Does this benefit stack with an existing Xbox Game Pass subscription? 

A: Xbox Game Pass Starter Edition is intended for Nitro members who do not already have an active Xbox Game Pass subscription. If you already subscribe to Xbox Game Pass, this benefit is notbe applicable.

Q: What counts toward my 10 hours of cloud gaming per month? 

A: Cloud gaming hours are counted from the time you begin streaming a session. Hours reset at the beginning of each calendar month.

Q. Can I play my own games with the 10 hours of cloud gaming?
A: Yes, if a game is cloud playable and you own it digitally with Xbox, you can stream it using your 10 hours of cloud gaming each month.

Q. Can I play from the library of 50+ games on my devices without using my 10 hours of cloud gaming?

A: Yes, you can download and play games on PC, Xbox console, and supported handhelds without impacting your 10 hour cloud gaming limit.

Q: Why does my benefit show as "unavailable"? 

A: If you're seeing "Unavailable," there may be a temporary issue generating access. No action is needed on your end — check back later to claim your benefit.

Q: I think I found a bug. How do I report it?

A: If you're experiencing technical issues or suspect you've encountered a bug, please reach out to Discord Support for help. We can investigate and assist you further.

Q: What happens to my discount code if I cancel my Nitro subscription?

A: Any codes you've already claimed will remain valid until their expiration date, but you won't receive new codes unless you resubscribe to Nitro.

Q: When do the discount codes expire?

A: It depends on the type of code. Monthly codes are valid through the end of the current month. One-time codes (such as for Logitech’s new products and the Pro Series sim line) do not expire and can be redeemed at any time.

Q: Are there any restrictions on the products I can purchase with the discount?

A: Yes, see above for individual product restrictions for each partner discount.

Q: Is there a limit to how many times I can use my discount code?

A: Each discount code can be used once, and you can only use one code per transaction on each website. However, you'll receive a new code each month your Nitro subscription is active.

Q: When exactly do new codes become available?

A: New discount codes become available at the beginning of each calendar month for active Nitro members.

Q: Can I use my code on any device?

A: Yes! Once you receive your code through the Discord desktop app, you can copy it and redeem it at the appropriate webstore on any device of your choice.

Q: Why does my discount code show as "unavailable"?

A: If you’re seeing “Unavailable”, that means there are issues generating discount codes. No action is needed on your end. Check back later to claim your code!

Unavailable Discount Code

Q: Why isn't my discount code working?

A: If you're experiencing issues with your Logitech G discount code, contact their support team directly: https://support.logi.com/hc/en-us 

If you're experiencing issues with your SteelSeries or KontrolFreek discount code, contact their support teams directly:

  • SteelSeries Support: https://support.steelseries.com/hc/en-us 
  • KontrolFreek Support: https://support.kontrolfreek.com/hc/en-us 

Q: I think I found a bug. How do I report it?

A: If you're experiencing technical issues or suspect you've encountered a bug, please reach out to Discord Support for help. We can investigate and assist you further.

articles.name: Tracker.gg x Discord Nitro Promo
articles.title: Tracker.gg x Discord Nitro Promo
articles.body:
Starting from March 16th, 2026 (10:00AM PST) to June 16th, 2026 (10:00AM PST), Tracker.gg users can claim 1 month of Discord Nitro. Learn below about how you can claim Discord Nitro.

Promotion Summary

Offer Details: 1 month of Discord Nitro

Who is Eligible: Users who have had an active Nitro subscription or Nitro trial in the last 12 months are not eligible to redeem this Nitro promotion. 

Note: This promotion is not eligible in the following regions: Syria, Ukraine, Russia, Iran, and North Korea. 
We may add additional restrictions from time to time, if you receive an "Unknown Gift Code" error during the claim/redemption process, you are not eligible to redeem this promotion. You can learn more at this Help Center article.

Users who have had a Discord Nitro subscription at any point on their accounts and have had a Nitro Classic or Nitro Basic subscription in the past 12 months are ineligible to redeem this promotion.

If a user has had a Nitro Basic, Nitro Classic, or Server Boosting subscription active on their account and they’ve never had a Nitro subscription, redeeming this promotion will override that subscription.

We will not be able to refund any leftover subscription time from those, and the user will be forfeiting the remainder of the existing subscription when activating the 1 month Nitro trial.

Users who have already claimed a gift from this promotion are not eligible to claim further gifts from this promotion.

Requirements: Claim through the Tracker.gg website or app when logged into your TRN account by June 16th, 2026 (10:00AM PST), and redeem in your Discord account by July 16th, 2026 (11:59PM PST).

Subscription: After the 1 month trial period, your Nitro subscription will convert to a monthly recurring subscription, and Discord will bill your payment method per month until you cancel it. You can cancel at any time in your Discord User Settings.

What this article covers: 

  • Promotion Summary
  • What is Discord Nitro?
  • What is Tracker.gg?
  • How do I claim the Nitro Promo from my TRN account?  
  • How do I redeem the Nitro Trial on my Discord Account? 
    • Eligibility for Redeeming the Nitro Trial
    • Step by Step Redeem Instructions
      • What if I am an Active Nitro Basic, Nitro Classic, or Server Boosting Subscriber? 
  • Frequently Asked Questions
    • Promotion Details
    • Eligibility for the Promotion
    • Payment Info and Subscription Questions
    • Potential Errors and Other Issues

What is Discord Nitro?

Discord Nitro is our subscription plan that gives you access to special perks that’ll make your time on Discord as fun as ever! Personalize and enhance your experience with popular perks like HD streaming, 500MB file uploads, profile and avatar customizations, 2 free Server Boosts, and more:

  • Custom Emojis
  • Discord App Color Themes
  • Super Reactions
  • Avatar Decorations
  • Unique Server Profiles
  • Server Boosts
  • More Nitro & Nitro Basic perks

What is Tracker.gg?

Tracker.gg is a leading gaming analytics platform operated by Tracker Network that provides players with real-time performance statistics, rankings, and insights across major competitive titles such as Valorant, Fortnite, Apex Legends, and Call of Duty. 

The platform aggregates match and player data to power detailed profiles, leaderboards, and progression tracking, helping gamers understand how they perform relative to peers and improve over time.

Learn more about Tracker.gg here.

How do I claim the Nitro Promo from my TRN account?

1. To claim your Discord Nitro promo code, simply head over to your Tracker.gg XP page. 

In the Tracker.gg site, TRN users must navigate to the XP page for the Discord Nitro promotion.

2. You'll see the "Discord Pack" waiting for you - simply press the "Open" button! 

3. Once unlocked, navigate to your rewards list, hit "Claim," and accept the Terms of Service. Your unique Nitro promo code will appear on your screen.

How do I redeem the Nitro Trial on my Discord Account?

Once you have claimed the promo, you'll now need to officially redeem this promo on your Discord account. You must redeem this Nitro trial by July 16th, 2026 (11:59PM PST).

Eligibility for Redeeming the Nitro Trial

Note: This promotion is not eligible in the following regions: Syria, Ukraine, Russia, Iran, and North Korea. 
If you receive an "Unknown Gift Code" error during the claim/redemption process, you are not eligible to redeem this promotion. You can learn more at this Help Center article.

Users who have had an active Nitro subscription or Nitro trial in the last 12 months are not eligible to redeem this Nitro promotion.

Users who have had a Discord Nitro subscription at any point on their accounts and have had a Nitro Classic or Nitro Basic subscription in the past 12 months are ineligible to redeem this promotion.

If a user has had a Nitro Basic, Nitro Classic, or Server Boosting subscription active on their account and they’ve never had a Nitro subscription, redeeming this promotion will override that subscription.

Users who have already claimed a gift from this promotion are not eligible to claim further gifts from this promotion.

Step by Step Redeem Instructions

If you're eligible and ready to redeem this Nitro subscription on your own Discord account, you can follow these steps below to redeem the promo.

Note:
- Before redeeming Nitro, make sure you are logged in your own Discord account.
- If you accidentally redeemed the Nitro promo on an account that is not yours, we are unable to provide you with another promo.

Step 1 - Logging into Discord

  • If you're not already logged into Discord in your browser, you will then be automatically redirected to either:

Create an account Page (Left) and Login Page (Right)

Step 2 - Claiming your promotion gift

Once you have logged in to your account, you will see a message asking you to claim your promotion gift.

The account you are currently logged in as will receive this gift. This gift cannot be shared with a friend and once claimed, the gift cannot be switched to a different user, so be sure you are on the account you want to claim the gift for. If you want to change to a different account, press on the Switch Accounts.

Press on Next when you’re ready to redeem your gift on your chosen account.

Claim your gifts! Window

Step 3 - Redeeming your promotion gift

Review the details of the promotion then press on Next.

Offer Confirmation Window

Note: If you switch to a different account at this step, you will not be able to claim and redeem the promotion on the account you switched to.

Make sure your payment information is accurate. If your Discord account does not have your payment information, please fill out your payment details. You will not be charged until the trial ends and the subscription renews.

Payment Information

When you've filled out all the necessary information and you're ready to redeem this promotion, press the confirmation button.

Step 4 - Subscription Now Activated

After accepting the promo to your account, you'll now be automatically redirected to your Subscriptions tab in your User Settings to see your newly activated Nitro subscription on your account.

What if I am an Active Nitro Basic, Nitro Classic, or Server Boosting Subscriber? 

Just to clarify - if you have had a Discord Nitro subscription at any point on your account and have had a Nitro Classic or Nitro Basic subscription in the past 12 months, you are ineligible to redeem this promotion.

So long as you have had a Nitro Basic, Nitro Classic, or Server Boosting subscription active on your account and you’ve never had a subscription, you will be able to redeem this promotion and replace it with the 1 month Nitro subscription.

Important: By choosing to activate this 1 month trial, you will lose any features associated with your current subscriptions, and **you will not receive a refund for any remaining value on your current subscription**.

At the end of the promotion period, you'll be converted to a paying Nitro subscription per month unless you cancel your subscription.

Frequently Asked Questions

We know there's a lot of information to unpack on this promotion above, so we've also broken down this information in this FAQ section as well too.

  • Promotion Details
  • Eligibility for the Promotion
  • Payment Info and Subscription Questions
  • Potential Errors and Other Issues
    • Already Had/Have Nitro
    • Already Redeemed
    • Something's Wrong Here

Promotion Details

Q: How long does this promotion run for?

A: Tracker.gg users can claim Discord Nitro promo between March 16th, 2026 (10:00AM PST) to June 16th, 2026 (10:00AM PST).

After June 16th, 2026 (10:00AM PST), Tracker.gg users will no longer be able to claim a trial promo.

Q: How long does the Discord Nitro promotion last for?

A: You must redeem the Nitro promotion by July 16th, 2026 (11:59PM PST).

If a code has not been redeemed to the Discord account by July 16th, 2026 (11:59PM PST), it will expire and you will no longer be able to have 1 month of Discord Nitro.

Eligibility for the Promotion

Q: Who can claim this promotion from their TRN account?

A: Tracker.gg users through their TRN accounts can claim 1 month of Discord Nitro.

Q: Who can redeem this Nitro trial to their Discord account?

A: Users who have never had an active Nitro subscription on their Discord accounts are eligible to redeem this Nitro promotion.

Additionally, users who previously had a Discord Nitro subscription and have had a Nitro Classic or Nitro Basic subscription in the past 12 months are ineligible.

This promotion is not available in the following regions: Syria, Ukraine, Russia, Iran, and North Korea. If you receive an "Unknown Gift Code" error during the claim/redemption process, you are not eligible to redeem this promotion. You can learn more at this Help Center article.

Q: What if I'm a current or previous Nitro Basic, Nitro Classic, or a Server Boost subscription? Can I also take part in this promotion? 

A: If you previously had a Discord Nitro subscription at any point on your accounts and have had a Nitro Classic or Nitro Basic subscription in the past 12 months, you are ineligible to redeem this promotion.

If you have had a Nitro Basic, Nitro Classic, or Server Boosting subscription active on your account and you’ve never had a Nitro subscription,  you will be able to redeem this promotion and replace it with the 1 month Nitro subscription.

Important: By choosing to activate this 1 month trial, you will lose any features associated with your current subscriptions, and **you will not receive a refund for any remaining value on your current subscription**.

At the end of the promotion period, you'll be converted to a paying Nitro subscription per month unless you cancel your subscription.

Payment Info and Subscription Questions

Q: Why do I need to submit my payment info to redeem Nitro?

A: Because Nitro is a subscription plan, at the end of your 1 month trial, we will start your first billing cycle so you can continue to enjoy Nitro on Discord.

However, you can cancel the Nitro subscription at any time before the end of the 1 month.

Q: Why do I see a temporary hold/charge on my account when this is supposed to be a free promotion?

A: Just to clarify, this Nitro promotion is free, however, there will be a temporary hold placed on the payment method upon activating the promotion. This is to help Discord confirm the payment method works as expected and to prevent the use of fraudulent payment methods. This charge will appear in your Discord transaction history, and it may appear as a hold on your payment method.

We immediately reverse the temporary hold, and you’ll see that reflected in your Discord transaction history. Your bank may take longer to remove the hold from your payment method.

Note: Banks will usually remove the temporary hold from your account within a few business days.

If your Discord transaction history does not show the hold released after 5 business days, please let us know at https://dis.gd/contact, and our support team will be able to investigate this issue further.

Potential Errors and Other Issues

Q: I see a "Oh no! It looks like you have Nitro already" error when I try to redeem my Nitro promo.

A: If you're seeing an "Oh no! It looks like you have Nitro already" error, that means that the Discord account you're trying to redeem this promotion on currently has a Nitro subscription so it is ineligible for this Nitro promotion.

Please make sure you're logged into the account that you want to redeem this promotion on.

Q: I see a "Sorry, looks like you’ve already had a Nitro sub!" error when I try to redeem my Nitro promo.

A: If you're seeing an “Sorry, looks like you’ve already had a Nitro sub” error, that means that you've previously subscribed to Nitro on your Discord account and you won't be able to redeem this 1 month perk.

If you have never subscribed to Nitro, please submit a ticket through our support system: https://dis.gd/contact and our support team will be able to investigate further.

Be sure to include your full Discord ID and email address tied to your Discord account.

Q: I see a "Looks like this gift has already been claimed" error when I try to claim my Nitro promo.

A: If you are seeing a "Looks like this gift has already been claimed" error, that means that the Nitro promo has already been used and cannot be used again. You may need to double check if you've claimed this promo on another Discord account.

Unfortunately, once the promo has been claimed, the promo cannot be re-enabled, and the Nitro cannot be transferred from one account to another.

Q: I see a "Wait a Second...Looks like this code didn't work." error when I try to redeem my Nitro promo.

A: If you're seeing an “Wait a Second...Looks like this code didn't work.” error, that means that the promo is invalid.

Please note that our support team is unable to intervene if you are given an invalid promo and we will not be able to provide a new promo.

Q: I see a “Looks like something went wrong” error when I click on “Next”.

A: There are several reasons why you may be seeing this error:

  • You are ineligible for this promotion
  • The promotion is invalid
  • The promotion has reached the maximum number of claims
  • The link is invalid

Please note that the Discord support team is not able to assist if you run into this error when attempting to redeem. See the list above for possible reasons why your account may be ineligible or otherwise unable to redeem this promotion.

Q: I see a "Sorry, This Payment Source Cannot Be Used for Redemption" error when trying to redeem my promo!

A: If you're seeing a "Sorry, This Payment Source Cannot Be Used for Redemption" error, that means that the payment method you've selected to cover the temporary $1 hold cannot be used for that purpose.

Please keep in mind that prepaid payment methods, such as Gcash, cannot be used to cover temporary holds due to the nature of how prepaid payment methods function.

For more information on temporary holds, please check out this article!

Q: I see a “Looks like this promotion has reached its maximum number of claims” error when trying to redeem my promo!

A: If you are seeing a “Looks like this promotion has reached its maximum number of claims” error, that means the maximum limit for claims of this promotion has been reached and this promotion can no longer be claimed. If you have claimed this Nitro promotion, you are still able to redeem it to your Discord account!

Please note that the support team is unable to assist you if you run into this error and we are not able to increase the limit of claims.

Q: I see a “Looks like your email isn’t verified. Make sure to verify your email before claiming this gift.”

A: If you are seeing a “Looks like your email isn’t verified, make sure to verify your email before claiming this gift” error, you are trying to redeem this Nitro promotion on a Discord account with an email that hasn’t been verified yet.

You can check out this support article on how to resend the email verification for your Discord account. If you need help setting up your Discord account, you can read our Getting Started article. 

Q: I’m having trouble with my TRN account.

A: Reach out to Tracker.gg’s support team: https://tracker.gg/support

articles.name: Age Assurance for Brazilian Users
articles.title: Age Assurance for Brazilian Users
articles.body:
Starting March 9th, 2026, we're rolling out age assurance in Brazil to comply with the Digital Statute for Children and Adolescents (Digital ECA), which takes effect on March 17th. This is part of a government regulation that applies to a wide range of digital services operating in Brazil.

When we delayed our global age assurance rollout on February 24th, we shared that we are still committed to meeting legal requirements in countries where they exist and Brazil is one of those countries. 

These changes bring updated default safety settings, age-gated access to certain content and spaces, and a privacy-forward age check, using k-ID, for adults who want to access age-restricted content or change certain default settings. Most users who don't access age-restricted content won't need to verify at all.

Our goal remains the same: apply the right protections for the right age groups while preserving the genuine community experience that makes Discord special. We will continue to update the Discord experience in Brazil to align with the Digital ECA and will tell you about those changes as we make them.

What this article covers:

  • What's Changing for Brazilian Users
  • Privacy-Forward Age Assurance
  • Default Safety Settings
    • Content Settings 
    • Social Settings
  • Frequently Asked Questions

What's Changing for Brazilian Users 

Starting March 17th, 2026, all Discord users in Brazil will have more protective settings to support teens. This means updated communications settings, restricted access to age-gated spaces, and content filtering. Brazilian users 18 and older can complete a privacy-forward age check to access age-restricted content and spaces, or to adjust relevant settings.

Privacy-Forward Age Assurance

Age assurance is the backbone of this new experience and is designed to respect your privacy and choice. You can choose to use facial age estimation or submit a form of identification and we plan to introduce more options in the future. This experience is powered by k-ID, our age assurance vendor.

Here's how we've built in privacy protections:

  • On-device processing: Video selfies for facial age estimation never leave your device
  • Quick deletion: Identity documents that go directly to k-ID are never seen by Discord. They are deleted immediately after age confirmation.
  • Straightforward age assurance: In most cases, you complete the process once and your Discord experience adapts to your verified age group. You may be asked to use multiple methods only when more information is needed to assign an age group.
  • Private status: Your age assurance status is only available to you and cannot be seen by others unless you choose to share it.

After completing verification, you'll receive a confirmation via direct message from our official Discord account. You can view your assigned age group at any time in My Account settings. If you'd like to appeal or retry the process, you can do that there too.

Note: If you receive an email or text message asking you to verify your age, it's not from us.  Age assurance only happens within Discord.

If you have not already confirmed your age, you will be prompted to complete an age check if you attempt to:

  • Unblur media with sensitive content flagged by our Sensitive Content Filter or attempt to change any of the default Sensitive Content Filter settings to “Show” sensitive content.
  • Turn off Message Requests (they are enabled by default to help you screen out unwanted DMs from your DM list).
  • Access age-restricted (18+) channels and servers.
  • Speak in a stage channel.
  • Toggle on age-restricted commands setting.
  • Toggle on “Allow access to NSFW servers on iOS” setting.
To learn more about how to confirm your age, go to our How to Complete Age Assurance on Discord article.

Default Safety Settings

All new and existing Brazilian users will have default safety settings. Age assurance may be required when you try to change certain settings or access specific content.

Content Settings 

  • Sensitive Content Filters are enabled by default. The default settings for this feature will blur content flagged in DMs with friends and messages in server channels, but it will also block content flagged in DMs with strangers. What’s changed is that modifying these default content settings to “Show” will now require age assurance.


 

Sensitive Content Filter Settings

  • Unblurring content flagged by our content filters will require age assurance.
  • Attempting to enter an age-restricted (18+) channel and age-restricted servers will require age assurance.

Social Settings

  • Message Requests are enabled by default and send all DMs from non-Friends to a separate space to help you review any incoming messages. Turning off Message Requests will require age assurance.

Message Requests Setting in Content & Social Page

  • You will be alerted before accepting a friend request from an unfamiliar user. Unfamiliar users are those you do not have any mutual friends or shared small servers (under 200 members) with. These alerts can help you make more informed decisions about new connections. These alerts cannot be turned off at this time.

Accept friend request? Alert

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Do these changes apply to all Brazilian Discord users?

A: Yes, the features and experiences described above apply to all new and existing Brazilian accounts.

Q: When do I need to go through age assurance?

A: You must be a confirmed adult to access age-restricted content and spaces or to modify certain safety settings. The majority of Discord users don’t access age restricted content and will never need to go through a facial age estimation flow or ID verification. 

Q: Are you going to be adding more verification method options?

A: We're actively working to add more verification options before we expand age assurance globally. However, some methods may not be available in certain regions that have set legal standards for what methods are acceptable.

Q: What age assurance methods are available?

A: There are two primary methods available right now:

  1. Facial Age Estimation (Video Selfie): Uses your device's camera to estimate your age group. Processing happens entirely on your device — your video selfie never leaves it, and Discord and our vendor, k-ID, never receives it. We only receive your age group.
  2. ID Scan: Scan your government-issued ID and take a quick selfie to confirm it matches. Your ID and selfie is processed to confirm your age and then deleted.

Note: You may need to complete both methods if we can't confirm your age group with sufficient confidence from facial age estimation alone.

We're actively working to add more verification options before we expand age assurance globally.

For step-by-step instructions on each method, see our How to Complete Age Assurance on Discord article.

Q: What if I choose not to confirm my age?

A: If you choose not to verify, here's exactly what happens: you keep your account, your servers, your friends list, your DMs, and voice chat. The only thing that changes is you won't be able to access age-restricted content or change certain default settings designed to protect teens. Nothing else about your Discord experience changes.

Q: How do you choose the vendors you work with for age assurance?

A: We're working on documenting every verification vendor and their practices on our website, and make it clear in the product who each vendor is. We've also set a new requirement: any partner offering facial age estimation must perform it entirely on-device. If they don't meet that bar, we won't work with them.

Q: How does Discord use my age assurance data?

A: We use this information for safety purposes and to deliver age-appropriate experiences on Discord. We will not use your age assurance information to target you with ads, and we don't sell your data. You can control how we use your data.

Q: Is my personal data stored during the age assurance process?

A: No. Discord and its trusted partners do not permanently store your personal identity documents or video selfies. Identity documents that go directly to k-ID are never seen by Discord. They are deleted immediately after age confirmation. Video selfies for facial age estimation are processed entirely on-device and never stored. Your identity is never associated with your Discord account.

Q: What are you getting from my ID or facial age estimation? What personal information do you receive?

A: Discord only gets your age. That’s it. Your identity is never associated with your account.

Q: What if my age assurance fails or shows incorrect results?

A: If the age assurance process fails or shows incorrect results, retry the verification through Discord system messages by selecting "Retry". 

If you were incorrectly identified as under minimum age requirement and want to appeal, please follow instructions here to use the ID scan option to verify your date of birth.

For more information on handling age assurance issues, visit our How to Complete Age Assurance on Discord article.

Q: Do I need to confirm my age every time I access age-restricted content or attempt to update a default setting?

A: No. Age assurance is typically a one-time process. In most cases, users complete the process once and their Discord experience adapts to their verified age group. Users may be asked to use multiple methods only when more information is needed to assign an age group.

Q: What happens if my age is confirmed as under 18?

A: If your age is confirmed as under 18, certain default safety settings will remain in place that cannot be changed. These include sensitive content filters, message request settings and restrictions when attempting to access age-restricted channels.

Q: What happens if my age is confirmed as too young to be on Discord?

A: Your account will be suspended if you are under the minimum age requirement to be on Discord. If you believe your verified age is incorrect, you may retry the verification process using the ID scan option. 

Please follow the instructions here to use the ID scan option to verify your date of birth.

Q: Can I appeal if I believe my account was incorrectly age-restricted or banned?

A: Yes. If you believe you were incorrectly verified and want to appeal, you may try the verification process using the ID scan option. 

Please follow the instructions here to use the ID scan option to verify your date of birth.

Q: How do content safety filters work?

A: Discord’s content safety filters are part of our broader Teen Safety Assist approach. They help reduce exposure to certain categories of potentially sensitive image-based media, especially for teens.

  • Mature Sexual Media Filter: Helps identify visual media that may contain sexually explicit or suggestive material involving adults
  • Graphic Media Filter: Helps identify image-based media that may contain violent or potentially disturbing visual material

These filters apply only to images and videos. They do not scan messages, voice, or calls, and they are designed to support age-appropriate experiences while preserving private conversations.

Q: Are there plans to expand these changes to other regions?

A: We're still working on expanding verification options, publishing our methodology for automatic age determination, and making sure every vendor we work with meets our privacy bar before we launch globally. Our global rollout is planned for the second half of 2026 as we build this thoughtfully. In the meantime, we may also move forward in regions if age assurance is required by regulation, in line with local laws and requirements.

articles.name: Profile Privacy Setting on Discord
articles.title: Profile Privacy Setting on Discord
articles.body:
With the Profile Privacy setting, you can control who has access to your profile details on Discord. When enabled, most profile details will be hidden from users who are not your friends or don't share a mutual server with you — ensuring your profile is only visible to the people you already have a connection with.

What this article covers:

  • What is Profile Privacy Setting?
  • How to Update Your Profile Privacy Setting
    • Desktop/Browser
    • Mobile
  • How Does This Affect Activity Sharing Setting
  • Frequently Asked Questions

What is Profile Privacy Setting?

Important: Profile Privacy setting is currently being tested and may not be available to all users.

Profile Privacy setting lets you control who sees your profile information. When enabled, certain details are hidden from anyone who isn't a friend or doesn't share a server with you. This setting can be accessed from desktop, browser, and mobile devices.

Profile Privacy setting will hide the following information:

  • Custom Status
  • Pronouns
  • Badges
  • Activity
  • Connected accounts
  • Bio
  • Widgets
  • Wishlists

When Profile Privacy setting is enabled, the following is still visible on your profile:

  • Avatar
  • Banner
  • Display name
  • Username
  • Server tag (when you share the same server)
  • Account age
  • Mutual friends and servers
     


 

Note: Users in the United Kingdom, Brazil, and Australia may see a different experience since we are currently testing variations in these regions.

How to Update Your Profile Privacy Setting

Desktop/Browser

1. Tap on the cogwheel [] in the bottom-left corner to access your User Settings.

2. Navigate to the Data & Privacy tab and scroll down to the Profile Privacy setting section.

Profile Privacy setting

3. Here you can choose between the following options:

  • Friends & All Servers: Your full profile will be visible to your friends and any servers you join.
  • Friends & Small Server Only: Your profile will be visible to all friends and to anyone in your servers with 200 or fewer members
  • Friends Only: Your profile will be visible to your friends only.

4. After selecting your preferred setting from the list above, your preference will be automatically saved.

Mobile

1. Tap on your avatar in the bottom-right corner.

2. Press on the cogwheel [] in the top-right corner.

3. Navigate to the Data & Privacy and then scroll down until you see Profile Privacy setting.

4. Here you can choose between the following options:

  • Friends & All Servers: Your full profile will be visible to your friends and any servers you join.
  • Friends & Small Server Only: Your profile will be visible to all friends and any servers you’re in that have 200 or less members.
  • Friends Only: Your profile will be visible to your friends only.

5. Tap on the setting you’d like to choose and then navigate out of your User Settings to save.
 

Profile Privacy setting on mobile

How Does This Affect Activity Sharing Setting

Profile Privacy setting and Activity Sharing are separate settings, but they work together to give you consistent control over your visibility on Discord.

When you change your Profile Privacy setting, you'll be prompted to update your Activity Sharing setting to match. For example, if you set your Profile Privacy setting to Friends Only, Discord will ask if you'd like to restrict your Activity Sharing to friends only as well. You can choose to update both settings at once, or decline and manage them independently.

Stop sharing activity too? window prompt

You can review and adjust your Activity Sharing setting at any time in User Settings > Activity Privacy. To learn more, see Activity Sharing on Discord.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Am I required to age assure in order to change this setting?

A: No. You can change this setting at any time in your Data & Privacy settings > Profile Privacy.

Q: If my profile is private, can people still send me friend requests? 

A: Yes. Making your profile private does not affect your ability to receive friend requests. Others can still send you a friend request even if they can't see your full profile details.

Q: If my profile is private, can people still send me DMs? 

A: Yes. You may control who can DM you through your Content & Social settings. Your profile privacy does not affect this setting.

Q: How does the Profile Privacy setting work for server moderators?
A: Giving users control over their profile information matters for safety, and that applies to everyone equally, moderators included. Moderators don't get different access to private profile details and will see the same info as any other member in your shared server. 

That said, moderators keep all their existing community management tools that they need to keep their communities safe. Username, display name, account age, and mutual server info stay visible no matter what.

articles.name: How to Share Feedback with Discord
articles.title: How to Share Feedback with Discord
articles.body:
We love hearing from our community! Whether you have a suggestion for a new feature, thoughts on a recent update, or general feedback about your Discord experience, our Feedback Form is one of the ways to share it with us.

Need help with your account or a specific issue?   
·  The Feedback Form is not monitored for support requests, and you will not receive a reply. 
·  If you need help with your account or want to request a refund, please visit dis.gd/contact and select the correct drop down option in the What can we help you with? section to reach our Support team.

What this article covers:

  • Feedback vs. Other Request Types — Which Option Should I Use?
  • How to Submit Feedback Through the Feedback Form
    • What Happens After You Submit Feedback?
  • Frequently Asked Questions
  • Still Need Help?

Feedback vs. Other Request Types — Which Option Should I Use?

Discord support requests (including feedback) are submitted through our Support Portal at dis.gd/contact.

When you arrive, you'll see a dropdown menu that asks: "What can we help you with?"

To submit feedback, select "Feedback" from the dropdown. This will load the Feedback Form, which is specifically designed to collect product suggestions, feature requests, and general thoughts about your Discord experience.

The Support Portal offers several request types to make sure your submission reaches the right team. Here's a breakdown:

Looking for safety resources? Visit the Discord Safety Center for information on staying safe on Discord.

How to Submit Feedback Through the Feedback Form

If you need support signing into your Discord Support Account, check out this article here.
  1. Sign into your Discord Support Account.
  2. Navigate to the Discord Feedback Form at dis.gd/feedback.
  3. Select the category that best describes your feedback (e.g., Text Chat, Voice and Video, etc.).
  4. Fill in the feedback fields with as much detail as you'd like to share.
  5. Check the acknowledgment box confirming you understand this form is for feedback only and does not receive support replies.
  6. Select Submit.

Once your form has been submitted, you'll receive a confirmation email letting you know your feedback was received.

What Happens After You Submit Feedback?

Once you submit your feedback, it's forwarded to the appropriate internal team at Discord — such as Product, Engineering, or Design — for review.

Here are a few things to keep in mind:

  • You will not receive a personal reply. Our Support team does not respond to submissions in the Feedback Form.
  • We can't guarantee implementation. While all feedback is read and valued, we're unable to provide an ETA on when — or whether — a suggestion will be acted on.
  • Your feedback matters. Even without a reply, your submission helps shape Discord's product decisions and roadmap.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Can I submit a bug report through the Feedback Form? 

A: If you've found a bug, the best way to get it resolved is to submit a support ticket at dis.gd/contact so our team can investigate. You're welcome to share broader product feedback about the experience, but bug reports requiring investigation should go through Support.

Q: Will Discord reply to my feedback in the Feedback Form? 

A: No. The Feedback Form is a one-way channel used to collect community input. While we cannot reply to individual submissions, we do review feedback internally and share it with the appropriate teams.

Q: What if I submitted a feedback request in the Support Form? 

A: Our Support team will redirect you to the Feedback Form so we can properly track this feedback.

Q: I submitted a legal inquiry or formal request through this Feedback Form — what should I do? 

A: The Feedback Form is not the appropriate channel for legal inquiries, notices, or demands, or regulatory complaints. Submissions to this form are not reviewed by Discord's legal team. Please refer to discord.com/terms or contact Discord through the appropriate legal channels.

Q: I submitted feedback but want to follow up or add more context. What should I do? 

A: You're welcome to submit a new feedback entry to the Feedback Form at any time. Since we don't reply to individual submissions, there's no way to expand to an existing submission — but every entry is reviewed.

Still Need Help?

  • If you have an account issue, billing question, or need hands-on support, our Support team is here for you - visit dis.gd/contact to submit a new ticket to our Support team.
  • If you need support signing into your Support account, visit this support article to learn more.
  • If you’d like to learn more about staying safe on Discord, visit the Discord Safety Center for more information.

articles.name: Age Assurance for Australian Users
articles.title: Age Assurance for Australian Users
articles.body:
Although Discord has not been classified as an Age-Restricted Social Media Platform under Australia’s Social Media Minimum Age Act, we decided to apply a similar experience to new and existing users in Australia starting December 9th, 2025. 

These defaults include automatic content filtering (which is already enabled for teens everywhere) to reduce the likelihood that teens encounter certain types of potentially harmful content, as well as different social settings. Australian users who wish to access content flagged by our filters or customize those settings can do so only after verifying that they are 18 or older through our new privacy-forward age assurance experience. 

Our new privacy-forward age assurance experience is required in specific scenarios (more details below), while building on our commitment to fostering genuine connections and a positive online experience. Check out this article to learn more about why we are making these changes in Australia.

Note: To check out additional Safety Resources for Australian Users go here.

What this article covers:

  • Privacy-Forward Age Assurance
  • Default Safety Settings
    • Content Settings
    • Social Settings
  • Frequently Asked Questions

Privacy-Forward Age Assurance

Age assurance is the backbone of this new experience and is designed to respect your privacy and choice. You can choose to use facial age estimation or submit a form of identification and we plan to introduce more options in the future. This experience is powered by k-ID, our age assurance vendor.

Here's how we've built in privacy protections:

  • On-device processing: Video selfies for facial age estimation never leave your device
  • Quick deletion: Identity documents that go directly to k-ID are never seen by Discord. They are deleted immediately after age confirmation.
  • Straightforward age assurance: In most cases, you complete the process once and your Discord experience adapts to your verified age group. You may be asked to use multiple methods only when more information is needed to assign an age group.
  • Private status: Your age assurance status is only available to you and cannot be seen by others unless you choose to share it.

If you have not already confirmed your age, you will be prompted to complete an age check if you attempt to:

  • Unblur media sensitive content flagged by our Sensitive Content Filter or attempt to change any of the default Sensitive Content Filter settings to “Show” sensitive content. that’s blurred by the Sensitive Content Filter.
  • Turn off Message Requests (they are enabled by default to help you screen out unwanted DMs from your DM list).
  • Access age-restricted (18+) channels and servers.
  • Speak in a stage channel.
  • Toggle on age-restricted commands setting.
  • Toggle on “Allow access to NSFW servers on iOS” setting.
Note: We are currently running experiments where users have to complete age assurance in order to speak in a Stage Channel.
To learn more about how to confirm your age, go to our How to Complete Age Assurance on Discord article.

Default Safety Settings

All new and existing Australian users will have enhanced default safety settings. Age assurance may be required when you try to change certain settings or access specific content.

Content Settings

  • Sensitive Content Filters are enabled by default. The default settings for this feature will blur content flagged in DMs with friends and messages in server channels, but it will also block content flagged in DMs with strangers. What’s changed is that modifying these default content settings to “Show” will now require age assurance.

Sensitive Content Filter Settings

  • Unblurring content flagged by our content filters will require age assurance.
  • Attempting to enter an age-restricted (18+) channel and age-restricted servers will require age assurance.

Social Settings

  • Message Requests are enabled by default to help you screen out unwanted DMs from your DM list. Turning off Message Requests will require age assurance.

Message Requests Setting in Content & Social Page

  • You will be alerted before accepting a friend request from an unfamiliar user. Unfamiliar users are those you do not have any mutual friends or shared small servers (under 200 members) with. These alerts can help you make more informed decisions about new connections. These alerts cannot be turned off at this time.

Accept friend request? Alert

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Do these changes apply to all Australian Discord users?

A: Yes, the features and experiences described above apply to all new and existing Australian accounts.

Q: When do I need to go through age assurance?

A: You must be a confirmed adult to access age-restricted content and spaces or to modify certain safety settings. The majority of Discord users don’t access age restricted content and will never need to go through a facial age estimation flow or ID verification. 

Q: Are you going to be adding more verification method options?

A: We're actively working to add more verification options before we expand age assurance globally. However, some methods may not be available in certain regions that have set legal standards for what methods are acceptable.

Q: What age assurance methods are available?

A: There are two primary methods available right now:

  1. Facial Age Estimation (Video Selfie): Uses your device's camera to estimate your age group. Processing happens entirely on your device — your video selfie never leaves it, and Discord and our vendor, k-ID, never receives it. We only receive your age group.
  2. ID Scan: Scan your government-issued ID and take a quick selfie to confirm it matches. Your ID and selfie is processed to confirm your age and then deleted.

Note: You may need to complete both methods if we can't confirm your age group with sufficient confidence from facial age estimation alone.

We're actively working to add more verification options before we expand age assurance globally.

For step-by-step instructions on each method, see our How to Complete Age Assurance on Discord article.

Q: What if I choose not to confirm my age?

A: If you choose not to verify, here's exactly what happens: you keep your account, your servers, your friends list, your DMs, and voice chat. The only thing that changes is you won't be able to access age-restricted content or change certain default settings designed to protect teens. Nothing else about your Discord experience changes.

Q: How do you choose the vendors you work with for age assurance?

A: We're working on documenting every verification vendor and their practices on our website, and make it clear in the product who each vendor is. We've also set a new requirement: any partner offering facial age estimation must perform it entirely on-device. If they don't meet that bar, we won't work with them.

Q: How does Discord use my age assurance data?

A: We use this information for safety purposes and to deliver age-appropriate experiences on Discord. We will not use your age assurance information to target you with ads, and we don't sell your data. You can control how we use your data.

Q: Is my personal data stored during the age assurance process?

A: No. Discord and its trusted partners do not permanently store your personal identity documents or video selfies. Identity documents that go directly to k-ID are never seen by Discord. They are deleted immediately after age confirmation. Video selfies for facial age estimation are processed entirely on-device and never stored. Your identity is never associated with your Discord account.

Q: What are you getting from my ID or facial age estimation? What personal information do you receive?

A: Discord only gets your age. That’s it. Your identity is never associated with your account.

Q: What if my age assurance fails or shows incorrect results?

A: If the age assurance process fails or shows incorrect results, retry the verification through Discord system messages by selecting "Retry". 

If you were incorrectly identified as under minimum age requirement and want to appeal, please follow instructions here to use the ID scan option to verify your date of birth.

For more information on handling age assurance issues, visit our How to Complete Age Assurance on Discord article.

Q: Do I need to confirm my age every time I access age-restricted content or attempt to update a default setting?

A: No. Age assurance is typically a one-time process. In most cases, users complete the process once and their Discord experience adapts to their verified age group. Users may be asked to use multiple methods only when more information is needed to assign an age group.

Q: What happens if my age is confirmed as under 18?

A: If your age is confirmed as under 18, certain default safety settings will remain in place that cannot be changed. These include sensitive content filters, message request settings and restrictions when attempting to access age-restricted channels.

Q: What happens if my age is confirmed as too young to be on Discord?

A: Your account will be suspended if you are under the minimum age requirement to be on Discord. If you believe your verified age is incorrect, you may retry the verification process using the ID scan option. 

Please follow the instructions here to use the ID scan option to verify your date of birth.

Q: Can I appeal if I believe my account was incorrectly age-restricted or banned?

A: Yes. If you believe you were incorrectly verified and want to appeal, you may try the verification process using the ID scan option. 

Please follow the instructions here to use the ID scan option to verify your date of birth.

Q: How do content safety filters work?

A: Discord’s content safety filters are part of our broader Teen Safety Assist approach. They help reduce exposure to certain categories of potentially sensitive image-based media, especially for teens.

  • Mature Sexual Media Filter: Helps identify visual media that may contain sexually explicit or suggestive material involving adults
  • Graphic Media Filter: Helps identify image-based media that may contain violent or potentially disturbing visual material

These filters apply only to images and videos. They do not scan messages, voice, or calls, and they are designed to support age-appropriate experiences while preserving private conversations.

Q: Are there plans to expand these changes to other regions?

A: We're still working on expanding verification options, publishing our methodology for automatic age determination, and making sure every vendor we work with meets our privacy bar before we launch globally. Our global rollout is planned for the second half of 2026 as we build this thoughtfully. In the meantime, we may also move forward in regions if age assurance is required by regulation, in line with local laws and requirements.

articles.name: Favorites FAQ
articles.title: Favorites FAQ
articles.body:
Keep the conversations that matter most right at your fingertips. With Favorites, Nitro users can add your most-used channels and DMs into a single, dedicated space. Whether it's a text channel or direct message you check constantly, Favorites puts everything important in one easy-to-reach place.

What this article covers:

  • What are Favorites?
  • How to Favorite a Channel or DM
    • How to Remove a Channel or DM from Favorites
    • How to Create a Category for your Favorites
  • How to View your Favorites
  • Frequently Asked Questions

What are Favorites?

Important: Favorites is currently available as a Nitro experiment on desktop and browser. Access may vary by user, and mobile support is limited to view-only.

Favorites is a feature that places all your top channels and DMs in a place that’s easy to find. Any channel or direct message you mark as a favorite will appear in a dedicated Favorites section at the top of your server list, keeping your most important conversations separate from the rest.

You can favorite channels from any server you're in, as well as DMs and group DMs — making Favorites a handy home base for everything you actually use on a regular basis. Plus, you can group these chats into custom categories to keep everything neat and tidy.

How to Favorite a Channel or DM

Important: Favorites are view-only on mobile. All management actions (editing, adding, moving, and renaming) require desktop or browser.

You can favorite text channels, direct messages (DMs), group direct messages (GDMs), forum channels, and voice channels. While you can’t favorite specific threads, you can favorite the text channel a thread is in. 

Tip: Even if you disable Favorites in Settings or unsubscribe and re-subscribe to Nitro, your Favorites will still be saved.

1. Navigate to the channel or DM/GDM that you want to favorite. 

2. Right-click on what you would like to favorite and then select Add to Favorite. If you have a Category set up, you can add the item to your preferred category instead.

Adding a text channel to Favorites

How to Remove a Channel or DM from Favorites

1. Navigate to your Favorites by selecting the Favorites icon [] in the top-left corner above your server list. 

2. Right-click on a channel or DM/GDM and select Remove from Favorites.

Removing a text channel from your Favorites

How to Create a Category for your Favorites

To organize your Favorites even more, you can create a variety of Categories. Uncategorized channels will appear at the top of your favorited items.

1. While under the Favorites tab right-click and select Create Category.

2. Name your Category and then press Create Category. 

3. Now when you add a channel or DM/GDM to your Favorites, you can add them into a category instead or drag your current favorites items to the category.

Create a Category for Favorites

How to View your Favorites

Tip: If you didn’t enable Favorites when you received the pop-up about the experiment, you will not see the Favorites icon  []. You will have to head to User Settings > Chat and enable or disable the Show Favorites Server toggle.

To view everything in your Favorites select the Favorite icon []  in the top-left corner above your server list on desktop, browser, or mobile. 


 

Favorites icon

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: What all can I add to my Favorites?

A: Text channels, voice channels, DMs, GDMs, and forum channels. Only threads can not be favorited.

Q: Does favoriting a channel notify anyone? 

A: No, it's personal and private.

Q: Is there a limit to how many items I can favorite?

A: You can add up to 200 items to your Favorites. This includes any Categories you create.

Q: Can I reorder my Favorites? 

A: Yes, you can move a favorited channel or DM, or organize them in a category.

Q: Are Favorites available on mobile?

A: Yes, Favorites is available on desktop, browser, and mobile. However, you can only edit Favorites from desktop/browser. 

Q: Can I preview Favorites if I don't have Nitro? 

A: Yes! You can preview the Favorites feature in settings even without a Nitro subscription. And if you are a past subscriber and re-subscribe, your Favorites are saved. For Nitro users, Favorites are saved even if you toggle the feature off — so when you turn it back on, everything will reappear exactly as you last set it up.

Q: Can I turn off Favorites from displaying?

A: Yes, if you are a Nitro user you can go to User Settings > Chat and enable or disable the Show Favorites Server toggle. Your Favorite will be saved even if you toggle it off or unsubscribe and re-subscribe to Nitro. 

Show Favorites Server toggle

articles.name: A/V E2EE Enforcement for Non-Stage Voice Calls
articles.title: A/V E2EE Enforcement for Non-Stage Voice Calls
articles.body:
What is the Update? 

Updated clients which support the DAVE (Audio & Video End-to-End Encryption) Protocol are now required to connect to non-stages voice calls across the Discord Platform.

If you attempt to connect with an out-of-date client, the voice gateway will reject your connection with close code 4017.

[Monitoring] Compatibility Mode Issue

Update as of March 3rd, 12:35PM PST

We've discovered that some users cannot update their Discord app on Windows because they have set Discord to "Compatibility Mode" and as a result get stuck on the last version of Discord that supported Windows 8. 

It is a persistent issue and any attempt to re-install the app will reinstall the old version.

How Can I Resolve This Issue? 

The only solution at the moment is to disable Compatibility Mode for the Discord executable and to fully restart the application so that the app picks up the update.

[Resolved] Chrome OS Device Issues

Update as of March 3rd, 1:30PM PST

We were investigating issues with some up-to-date ChromeOS devices connecting to voice channels. Our team has fixed the issue, and this issue should now be resolved.

If you were experiencing this issue, please restart your app or device in order to receive the update. 

For More Information

See our Help Center article for Minimum Client Version Requirements and End to End Encryption for Audio and Video for further details.

For more information and updates, head to our status page at https://discordstatus.com/.

articles.name: How to Report Non-Consensual Intimate Imagery Under The Take It Down Act
articles.title: How to Report Non-Consensual Intimate Imagery Under The Take It Down Act
articles.body:
Discord strictly prohibits non-consensual intimate imagery (NCII) and works to identify, remove, and prevent its spread across the platform. This article covers how people in the United States can file a non-consensual intimate imagery report under the Take It Down Act (TIDA), one of several tools available for the removal of NCII on Discord.

Note: Individuals who are not able to make a report under TIDA may submit reports of NCII through our standard in-app reporting system. Our non-consensual intimate imagery policy applies globally in addition to other legal rights you may have where you live.

What this article covers:

  • What Can Be Reported
  • How to Submit a Report
    • Accessing the Report Form
    • Required Information
    • How to Find Content Links for Your Report
    • Message Attachments
    • Third Party Links / Embedded Media in Messages
    • Emojis
    • Stickers
    • User Profiles (Avatars, Banners)
    • Server-related Images (Avatars, Banners)
  • What to Expect After You Submit a Removal Request Under TIDA
  • Frequently Asked Questions
Important: Need immediate help? If you have concerns about your personal safety, or the safety of someone you know, please contact local law enforcement or social services.

What Can Be Reported

If you are in the United States you can report content under TIDA that:

  • Shows you (or someone you're authorized to represent) nude or engaged in sexually explicit conduct
  • Was shared without your consent, or was captured when you had a reasonable expectation of privacy
  • Is either authentic, AI-generated, or digitally manipulated depicting you

This includes images or videos you may have originally consented to create but did not consent to have shared. There's no time limit—you can report content regardless of when it was created or posted.

Important: Please note that intimate imagery of a person under the age of 18 constitutes Child Sexual Abuse Material (CSAM). We report illegal child sexual abuse material and grooming to the National Center for Missing & Exploited Children (NCMEC). Users who post this content are permanently banned from Discord.

How to Submit a Report

Reports under TIDA must be submitted through our public report webform found at discord.com/report.

Within 48 hours of a report being submitted, we’ll review the report and take appropriate action. You will be notified via email on the status of your report.

Reports may be submitted by the depicted individual or an authorized representative (e.g. a parent, guardian, attorney) of the depicted individual. Multiple reports are allowed - please create one report for each piece of media.

Accessing the Report Form

1. On desktop or mobile devices, navigate to discord.com/report on your preferred browser

2. Follow the steps in the form to file a report.

Required Information

We recognize that reporting non-consensual intimate imagery can be difficult. Your report will remain confidential, and we will not disclose your information to the person who uploaded the imagery of you.  

To file a report under the Take It Down Act, the following information is required by law:

  • Legal name of the person that is depicted in the reported content
  • Email address where you wish to receive updates on your report
